input.question: How many courses have no prerequisites vs at least one?

payload.question: How many courses have no prerequisites vs at least one?

output.reasoning: Count courses grouped by whether they have any prerequisite rows (joined via prerequisites.course_id -> courses.id) and present two labeled buckets with deterministic ordering.
output.sql:
WITH course_pr_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    COUNT(p.prerequisite_course_id) FILTER (WHERE p.prerequisite_course_id IS NOT NULL) AS prereq_count
  FROM public.courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS p ON p.course_id = c.id
  GROUP BY c.id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisite' ELSE 'has_prerequisite' END AS prereq_status,
  COUNT(*) AS course_count
FROM course_pr_counts
GROUP BY CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisite' ELSE 'has_prerequisite' END
ORDER BY CASE WHEN prereq_status = 'no_prerequisite' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END;
assumptions: prerequisites.course_id references courses.id so a course has at least one prerequisite iff there is ≥1 row in prerequisites with that course_id

payload.sql:
WITH course_pr_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.id AS course_id,
    COUNT(p.prerequisite_course_id) FILTER (WHERE p.prerequisite_course_id IS NOT NULL) AS prereq_count
  FROM public.courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN public.prerequisites AS p ON p.course_id = c.id
  GROUP BY c.id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisite' ELSE 'has_prerequisite' END AS prereq_status,
  COUNT(*) AS course_count
FROM course_pr_counts
GROUP BY CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'no_prerequisite' ELSE 'has_prerequisite' END
ORDER BY CASE WHEN prereq_status = 'no_prerequisite' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END;
